text.title: GitLab CI/CD: Security and Secrets Rules
text.summary: Rules for protected variables, security template inclusion, container scanning, and secret detection in GitLab CI pipelines.

rule.statement: Mark all secret CI/CD variables as Protected (available only on protected branches) and Masked (hidden in job logs).
rule.rationale: Unprotected variables are accessible in any branch pipeline, including attacker-controlled branches. Unmasked variables appear in job logs visible to all project members.

rule.statement: Never echo or print secret variables in pipeline scripts; use `--silent` flags with curl and avoid `set -x` in scripts that handle secrets.
rule.rationale: Shell debug mode (`set -x`) prints every command with expanded variable values to the job log, exposing secrets to anyone with log access.

rule.statement: Use file-type CI/CD variables for certificates, kubeconfig, and private keys — they are written to a temp file and the path is the variable value.
rule.rationale: Multiline secrets in string variables are fragile across shells. File-type variables handle encoding correctly and are automatically cleaned up after the job.

rule.statement: Include GitLab security templates via `include:` rather than installing scanners manually — they receive automatic updates from GitLab.
rule.rationale: Manually maintained scanner installations drift in version and configuration over time. GitLab's managed templates stay current with the scanner vendor's latest recommendations.

example.note: Include all four GitLab security scanning templates.
example.code: include: - template: Security/SAST.gitlab-ci.yml - template: Security/Dependency-Scanning.gitlab-ci.yml - template: Security/Container-Scanning.gitlab-ci.yml - template: Security/Secret-Detection.gitlab-ci.yml

antipattern.description: Hardcoding credentials or API keys in `.gitlab-ci.yml`.
antipattern.reason: `.gitlab-ci.yml` is committed to Git. Even if the repository is private, any team member or compromised account can read the file and extract credentials.
